seoTitle: How to Use Time Tracking in Monday.com (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Log hours, automate timers, and build a productivity dashboard using Monday.com's Time Tracking column for accurate billable hours.
intro: Manual timesheets are easy to forget and hard to reconcile at invoicing time. This guide covers Monday.com's built-in Time Tracking column, from starting and pausing timers on task rows to automating them with status changes, and exporting clean reports for client billing or payroll.

steps.heading: Add the Time Tracking column
steps.body: Add and configure the official Time Tracking Column on any operational board.

steps.heading: Start, pause, and edit time logs
steps.body: Use the column to start, pause, and manually edit time logs directly inside task rows or subitems.

steps.heading: Review detailed history logs
steps.body: Check the history logs to see exactly which team members contributed hours to an individual item.

steps.heading: Automate timers with status changes
steps.body: Set up automations to automatically start or stop the tracking timer when a status shifts, such as triggering the clock when a task moves to "In Progress."

steps.heading: Track time across subitems
steps.body: Extend time tracking to subitems for complex, multi-step project workflows.

steps.heading: Build a productivity dashboard
steps.body: Use the Time Tracking Widget to visualize total logged hours versus budget caps, then export clean reports for billing or payroll.

faq.question: Does the timer start automatically when work begins?
faq.answer: You can automate this by setting a rule that starts the timer when a task's status shifts to something like "In Progress."

faq.question: Can I see who logged time on a task?
faq.answer: Yes, the history logs show exactly which team members contributed hours to a specific item.

faq.question: Can I export time data for invoicing?
faq.answer: Yes, you can export time tracking report summaries into Excel or CSV format for client invoicing and payroll processing.

faq.question: Does time tracking work on subitems too?
faq.answer: Yes, time can be tracked across subitems for complex, multi-step workflows.
